question: Let us return to the birth weight illustrative data concerning 10 SIDS cases. We have established that $\bar{x}=2890.5$ grams and $s=720.0$. Confidence intervals for $\mu$ at $90 \%, 95 \%$, and $99 \%$ levels of confidence will be calculated.
answer:
- The standard error of the mean $S E_{\bar{x}}=\frac{720}{\sqrt{10}}=227.684$.
- $d f=10-1=9$.
- For $90 \%$ confidence, use $t_{9,95}=1.833$ [Table C], so the $90 \%$ confidence interval for $\mu$ is $\bar{x} \pm\left(t_{n-1,1-\xi}\right)\left(S E_{\bar{x}}\right)=2890.5 \pm (1.833)(227.684)=2890.5 \pm 417.3=(2473.2$ to 3307.8$)$ grams.
- For $95 \%$ confidence, use $t_{9, .975}=2.262$, so the $95 \%$ confidence interval for $\mu$ is $2890.5 \pm(2.262)(227.684)=2890.5 \pm 515.1=$ (2375.4 to 3405.6 ) grams.
- For $99 \%$ confidence, use $t_{9, .995}=3.250$, so the $99 \%$ confidence interval for $\mu$ is $2890.5 \pm(3.250)(227.7)=2890.5 \pm 740.0=$ (2150.5 to 3630.5) grams.
